none.justification: It correctly identifies that the substantive scan-trigger logic and most failure conditions live in driver_nl80211_scan.c, but it misses the actual function in driver_nl80211.c that the GT requires: driver_nl80211_scan2, the no-logic forwarding wrapper. It also overstates nl80211_cmd_msg as the relevant driver_nl80211.c answer, so it cannot receive full credit.

ccodegraph.justification: It explicitly states the scope-trap result: driver_nl80211.c has only driver_nl80211_scan2, a pure forwarding wrapper with no independent error conditions, and the real logic is in the separate driver_nl80211_scan.c translation unit. The listed downstream conditions match the GT context without losing the file-scope distinction.

codegraph.justification: It cleanly identifies driver_nl80211_scan2 in driver_nl80211.c as the only relevant wrapper and says it has no error conditions of its own. It then correctly places and enumerates the actual scan-trigger failure paths in driver_nl80211_scan.c.

cbm.justification: It correctly flags that driver_nl80211.c contains only the thin driver_nl80211_scan2 wrapper and that the real scan-trigger logic is in the separate driver_nl80211_scan.c file, not textually included. Its failure-condition list aligns with the GT, including build failures, P2P rate-mask failures, and kernel rejection/AP retry subpaths.
